rulings.Case Title: JOHN WILKINS VS. MARY BAYTOP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Sale Of Dwelling
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 27, 2011, line 1. Order To Show Cause Re Sale Of Dwelling. The Order to Show Cause is off calendar. The court did not receive courtesy copies of the moving papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANN GARRISON VS. ONE WEST BANK et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 27, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANT NDEX WEST, LLC, ONEWEST BANK, FSB, DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY MOTION TO STRIKE 5TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend to file a 6th amended complaint that simply pleads the breach of contract cause of action. The 5th amended complaint confusingly includes causes of action that the Court previously dismissed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: AT&T ADVERTISING, L.P. VS. PATRICIA CRAMER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Written Interrogatories And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 27, 2011, Line 1, PLAINTIFF AT&T ADVERTISING, L.P. Notice And Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Written Interrogatories And Sanctions The motion is granted. No opposition was filed. Defendant shall respond to the interrogatories and pay $590 in sanctions by January 31, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mm

rulings.Case Title: BEN B.S. LEE et al VS. VINCENT MARK RAFANELLI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Defts To Provide Further Responses To Demands For Production, To Produce Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 27, 2011, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS BEN LEE AND SUSAN LEE'S Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Defts To Provide Further Responses To Demands For Production, To Produce Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions - The motion is denied without prejudice and the request for sanctions is denied. The parties are ordered to meet and confer meaningfully regarding the original objections and supplemental responses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MM

rulings.Case Title: SARAH JOHNSTON VS. ANON EVENTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Deft'S Answer To Pltf'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 27, 2011, line 5. DEFENDANT ANON EVENTS, INC. Motion To Amend Defendant'S Answer To Plaintiff'S Complaint. The motion to amend the answer to make the name change is granted. The Court also gives plaintiff leave to amend to add Anon Events aka Joegh Bullock as Doe One. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS A. SMITH et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 27, 2011, line 6. DEFENDANT WILLIAM GARLOCK, GFT PROPERTIES LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is continued to January 5, 2012 because (1) the opposition was untimely and (2) the Court is hearing an OSC in this matter on that date. Defendant to file and personally serve his reply by December 30, 2011. Defendant to provide a courtesy copy to department 302 per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)
